item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don’t know what the hell I just read but I liked it a lot (I’m kidding, I get the overall vibe of abuse)

Evocative as hell and disturbingly funny in parts

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The monster is just a metaphor for domestic abuse. Ezekiel is a reality bender and he created the monster to excuse his dad of his behavior.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you read the authoe's comment under spoiler on the first page of comments?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but is the SCP just some nightmare monster the kid makes up?

So does he fall prey to his own imagination?

Why aren't the parents scared of it and feed it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept is good, but I'm no-voting because of the format. There are several little issues with tone, there are typos, and sometimes the story becomes a little confusing.

It is clear that the author tried to capture the images on their mind, but didn't thought of it as text.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It bother me that the description is written in the past tense

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goosebumps, chills, whatever you want to call them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what the shit is henflesh??? btw sorry if a comment like this breaks some kind of rule

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, just as I've liked all the others in this group, but without reading 2782 I wouldn't have come to the conclusion that Ezekiel is the one causing it all. It could, perhaps, be a little clearer?

Also, in some places there are slight oddities or minor errors with grammar and phrasing. If acceptable to the author, I am happy to attempt to clean these up. I shan't do anything without permission, of course.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty sure this is the newest article I've read. I only ever access the wiki on my phone and don't know how to search for new articles aside from clicking anything on the mainlist that I can't remember. Also +1 for making me shudder.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Potentially spoilery question here
Make it go away!

So is the Ezekiel kid here the same person mentioned in 2782 as heading up the incarnation experiments (though significantly older there)? A screwed-up childhood due to abuse and unconscious reality manipulation could certainly account for his later…esoteric tendencies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Somehow I had completely missed the other articles related to 1782 and now after having gone through them and this one I really really like this. Is the green hummingbird something to do with the "green doves"/kakapos? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah I wasn't sure about that, thanks for explaining. I think this is the first neutralized SCP I've seen so it seemed out of place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There was far too little going on in the description, and far too many typos, for me to want to continue reading the addenda.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What the fuck. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is exceptionally good! Besides some formatting and grammar issues it's great. Also some personal bias for living near it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reasons I feel like this would be better as a tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want spoilers please
– hide block

Ezekiel bends reality subconsciously. The monster is an excuse for his dad's abusive behavior.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, couldn't find a place to put it though. I imagine with the thing dying everything goes back to normal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I got a "This House Has People In It" vibe from the camera footage. I think it works, even with the select few cliches that occur.

Overall, a refreshing surreal horror story. And was Ezekiel taken from the home while in flesh cube form?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been around a long time, and it takes a lot for a skip to really get to me. But jesus fucking christ, there's something wrong with this one.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gave me Henflesh.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-EX is for Explained articles, not Neutralized. An anomaly that dies or stops functioning anomalously would be considered neutralized, and an anomaly that was considered to be anomalous at one point (but it never was in the first place) is explained.

Edit: tomato ninja'd me :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe the proper term for neutralized SCPs is SCP-XXXX-EX.

Actually, Neutralized and Explained are two different classes. Neutralized is for things that have ceased displaying anomalous properties, while Explained is for things that were revealed to non-anomalous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea but there are little things that turn me off. I believe the proper term for neutralized SCPs is SCP-XXXX-EX. The context around this is a little unique so getting some feedback would benefit you.

Overall I like it, but it's apparent you didn't post to the forums for feedback and that's bad. It's apparent that this is a first draft but I think if it were reworked it could find a place here.

For now I'm not voting up or down.

EDIT: Seems like there's a lot of positive feedback in here. I'll admit I didn't read the entire thing because I was turned off pretty quickly, but it seems like if you do give it a full read it's rewarding. I can respect that.
